latest.title: Rule 2.9 Announcement
latest.content:
Today 15:40
RNS Number : 4829D
Audioboom Group PLC
07 May 2026
7 May 2026
Audioboom Group plc
("Audioboom"
or the "Company"
)
Rule 2.9 Announcement
In accordance with Rule 2.9 of the
City Code on Takeovers and Mergers, Audioboom confirms that, as at the date of this announcement, its issued ordinary share capital consists of
18,032,988
ordinary shares of no par value ("Ordinary Shares"). This follows the issue of 48,333 new Ordinary Shares which have been issued to satisfy the exercise of existing share options under the Company's Share Option Scheme 2014 by non-PDMR ex-employees. The International Securities Identification Number for Audioboom's Ordinary Shares is
JE00BJYJFG60
.
The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of Ordinary Shares and voting rights in the Company is 18,032,988. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries:
Audioboom Group PLC
Tel: +44(0)300 303 3765
Stuart Last, Chief Executive Officer
Brad Clarke, Chief Financial Officer
J Goodwin & Co (Financial Advisers to Audioboom)
Tel: +44(0)20 3976 6215
Rupert Hill / Oscar Koenig
Rockefeller Capital Management (Financial Advisers to Audioboom)
Tel: +1 212-549-5341
William B. Drewry / Francisco A. Mato
Cavendish Capital Markets Ltd (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Tel: +44(0)20 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams / Fergus Sullivan / Elysia Bough
About Audioboom
Audioboom is a global leader in podcasting - our shows are downloaded and viewed 170 million times each month by more than 50 million unique listeners around the world. Audioboom is ranked as the fifth largest podcast publisher in the US by Edison Research.
Audioboom's ad-tech and monetisation platform underpins a scalable content business that provides commercial, distribution, marketing and production services for a premium network of top tier podcasts. Key partners include the official Formula 1 podcasts 'F1: Beyond the Grid' and 'F1 Nation', 'True Crime Obsessed' (US), 'The Tim Dillon Show' (US), 'No Such Thing As A Fish' (UK) and 'The Cycling Podcast' (UK).
Audioboom operates internationally, with global partnerships across North America, Europe, Asia and Australia. The platform distributes content via Apple Podcasts, YouTube, Spotify, Pandora, Amazon Music, Google Podcasts, iHeartRadio, Facebook and Twitter as well as a partner's own websites and mobile apps.
For more information, visit
www.audioboom.com
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. company_30s.what_they_do: Audioboom Group plc owns and runs a cloud‑based platform that lets podcasters host, distribute and monetise their shows.  The service offers analytics, advertising solutions and tools for creators to grow audiences.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Podcasts now reach more listeners than any other audio medium and are a key advertising channel for brands.  Audioboom’s technology gives it a foothold in a market that is still expanding and where a few dominant platforms can command premium pricing.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been in a restructuring phase.  It recently completed a court case, issued a strategic review and posted a Q1 trading update that highlighted cost‑cutting measures.  The business is still generating modest revenue streams while trying to shore up its balance sheet.

rns_narrative: The firm has wrapped up a legal dispute that had been dragging on for months, signalling that one major uncertainty is gone.
A strategic review was announced, suggesting management is looking at new growth levers and potentially a change in business model.
Quarterly updates show the company is tightening spending but still struggling to hit revenue targets.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence‑to‑Hope: The chatter is quiet, but a few voices are optimistic about a possible takeover or a sharp rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to a potential bid above £10 and a clean legal record, arguing the market has over‑punished the stock and that a fresh capital injection could revive the platform.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s revenue pipeline is thin, the podcast market is crowded, and the legal case may have left lasting reputational damage.  They see the current price as a reflection of fundamental weakness.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from passive indifference to cautious optimism after the court case closed.  Few new posts appear, but those that do are bullish, indicating a small, hopeful minority.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat.  The lack of search chatter means there is no built‑in hype, leaving room for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the strategic review and a clear action plan could attract investor attention.
A formal takeover offer or a partnership announcement would instantly lift the stock.
A significant uptick in trading volume that exceeds 2× the 30‑day average would signal renewed interest.
Positive earnings guidance or a better-than‑expected Q2 report would validate the company’s turnaround.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in volume accompanied by a break of the 20‑day moving average.  Also monitor any press release that mentions a new advertiser or a partnership with a major media player.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still in the early stages of a deep‑crash cycle – the price has fallen far below its peak and the market has largely forgotten it.  A mean‑reversion move would likely start once institutional buyers begin to see value at the low end.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits 86 % below its all‑time high, offering a massive upside if it recovers to 50 % of that peak – that’s a 200 % gain from today’s price.  A takeover at £10 would be a 400 % return.  The company’s legal risk is resolved and the market has no reason to price in a rapid rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the strategic review fails to produce a clear growth path, revenue may stay flat and the platform could lose market share to larger competitors.  The worst case is a further 10 % slide before the price bottoms out, which would still be a 20 % loss from today’s level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is at least 200 % with a maximum realistic downside of 10 %.  The risk/reward ratio is roughly 20:1, a classic asymmetric bet.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average, size the position for 5 % of your portfolio, and set a stop just below the 50‑day average.  Use a trailing stop at 15 % below the peak once the price moves above the 50‑day average.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for volume to double the 30‑day average and a clear statement from management about a new partnership or a takeover offer.  That will be your trigger to go long.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or a major legal scandal emerges, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash, legally cleared podcast platform with a quiet market and a potential takeover offer is a prime contrarian play that offers a 200 % upside with a very small downside if the mean‑reversion thesis holds.
